additional_metadata.note: First Budapest dbt Meetup, jointly organized with the Budapest Big Data & Analytics Meetup group; livestreamed.

events.agenda: Inaugural Budapest dbt Meetup covering agile data warehouse development with open source tooling and building super-performant interactive dashboards with dbt, Firebolt, and Looker.
events.event_description: First dbt Meetup in Budapest, organized by Hiflylabs, shaping Hungarian dbt community history.
events.raw_text:
Tue, May 10, 2022 · 6:00 PM to 8:30 PM CEST

Hubhub, Budapest

Budapest dbt Meetup (first edition)

Speakers:
Andras Zimmer, CTO, Hiflylabs - Two-week dev cycles in a DW -- agility is reality with open source. How dbt and its ecosystem alleviate traditional data warehouse workflow pain points.

Mark Rittman, CEO, Rittman Analytics & Son N. Nguyen, Analytics Engineer, Hiflylabs - Super-performant interactive dashboards on huge datasets with dbt, Firebolt and Looker.

talks.description: How dbt and its ecosystem alleviate the painful workflows of traditional data warehouse and analysis processes, enabling two-week dev cycles.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 🎤Andras Zimmer CTO, Hiflylabs. Two-week dev cycles in a DW -- agility is reality with open source
talks.title: Two-week dev cycles in a DW -- agility is reality with open source

talks.description: Using dbt with Firebolt (a sub-second-response data warehouse technology) and Looker to build a fast, interactive dashboard on the Hacker News public dataset.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 🎤 Mark Rittman CEO, Rittman Analytics & Son N. Nguyen, Analytics Engineer, Hiflylabs. Super-performant interactive dashboards on huge datasets with dbt, Firebolt and Looker
talks.speaker_title: CEO, Rittman Analytics & Analytics Engineer, Hiflylabs
talks.title: Super-performant interactive dashboards on huge datasets with dbt, Firebolt and Looker

events.agenda: Talks on migrating from stored procedures to dbt during a cloud migration, and treating BI as code to boost analytics engineer productivity.
events.event_description: dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data, with talks predominantly focused on community members' experience with dbt.
events.location: The Spot Budapest, Király utca 26, Budapest
events.raw_text:
Thu, Nov 10, 2022 · 6:00 PM to 8:30 PM CET

The Spot Budapest, Budapest

Budapest dbt Meetup (in-person)

Presentation #1: From Stored Procedures to dbt - Zsombor Földesi, Senior Analytics Engineer, Hiflylabs. Migrating existing on-premise DWHs to the Cloud while adopting dbt, refactoring legacy SQL to dbt.

Presentation #2: BI as code: How to 10x your productivity as an analytics engineer - Oliver Laslett, Co-Founder & CTO, Lightdash. Latest developer tooling for BI and data, including automating deployments and preview environments.

talks.description: The process of migrating on-premise data warehouses to the cloud while adopting dbt, including best practices for refactoring legacy SQL code into dbt models.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 🗣️Presentation #1: From Stored Procedures to dbt. Speaker: Zsombor Földesi, Senior Analytics Engineer, Hiflylabs
talks.title: From Stored Procedures to dbt

talks.description: Modern developer tooling for BI and analytics engineers, covering automated deployments, preview environments, and code generation for BI.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 🗣️Presentation #2: BI as code: How to 10x your productivity as an analytics engineer. Speaker: Oliver Laslett, Co-Founder & CTO, Lightdash
talks.title: BI as code: How to 10x your productivity as an analytics engineer

Tue, Feb 21, 2023 · 6:00 PM to 8:30 PM CET

The Spot Budapest, Budapest

Budapest dbt Meetup (in-person)

Topic: Panel discussion about the most impactful predictions on the Modern Data Stack for 2023.

Panelists: Márton Hubay (Co-founder & CTO, Infinite Lambda), Bence Arató (Managing Director, BI Consulting), András Zimmer (Head of Analytics Engineering, Hiflylabs).

Host comment: Topics included predictions on Generative AI coding, dbt in the enterprise mainstream, cloud, and SaaS cost optimization, and market consolidation in the Modern Data Stack ecosystem.

talks.description: Panel discussion predicting key trends in the Modern Data Stack for 2023, covering generative AI in coding, enterprise dbt adoption, cloud/SaaS cost optimization, and market consolidation.

Budapest dbt Meetup (in-person)

Presentation #1: How Python on Databricks gives antigravity to dbt? - Zsombor Földesi, Lead Data Engineer, Hiflylabs. dbt's Python models using Databricks as a warehouse.

Presentation #2: Do you really need real-time data? - Ben Kulcsar, Senior Data Engineer, Hotjar. Deciding between batch and real-time pipelines using the modern data stack.

Wed, Sep 25, 2024 · 6:30 PM to 8:30 PM CEST

Create26, Budapest

Budapest dbt Meetup (in-person)

Presentation #1: Switching the query engine under a dbt project - Lessons from the migration trenches - Laci Pataki, Senior Data and Analytics Engineer. Moving dbt projects across platforms like Redshift, Snowflake, and Databricks.

Presentation #2: How to supercharge your dbt Testing - Best Practices & Pitfalls - Juan Perafan, Analytics Engineer consultant at Xebia, co-author of "Fundamentals of Analytics Engineering".

talks.description: Lessons learned from migrating dbt projects across query engines/platforms like Redshift, Snowflake, and Databricks, and how to keep a dbt project flexible for future migrations.
